scope_note: QA Whittaker Rational Direction S1 Cert family [266]: Layer 1 of the Whittaker -> QA development ladder (see docs/specs/QA_WHITTAKER_RATIONAL_DIRECTION_CERT_DRAFT.md sec. 8). CLAIM (narrow): the QA-rational direction set D_m on the unit circle S^1, defined by D_m = { (C/G, F/G) : (b,e) in {1..m}^2, gcd(b,e)=1, d=b+e, a=b+2e, C=2*d*e, F=a*b=d^2-e^2, G=d^2+e^2 }, is finite, exactly enumerable in integer arithmetic, and admits two structural theorems on the closed first quadrant of S^1: (W2) for any two distinct directions, sin(angular separation) >= 1/(G_i*G_j) >= 1/G_max(m)^2, proven from cross-product integrality (all-pairs validator check); (W3) for any L-Lipschitz angular profile g on [0, pi/2], nearest-neighbor sampling at D_m^+ := D_m union {E_0=(1,0), E_inf=(0,1)} (boundary anchors are observer-side only, NOT QA seeds, NOT counted in |D_m|, NOT in W1, NOT in W2) yields sup error <= L*Delta_max^+(m), where Delta_max^+(m) is the maximum angular gap on the closed quadrant. Predictions verified for m in {9, 24, 72}: |D_m| = 55, 359, 3175. The cert does NOT prove Whittaker's wave-equation theorem, Maxwell's equations, electromagnetism, two-scalar-potential reductions (Whittaker 1904), or any physics; Whittaker 1903 is motivation only. Layer 2 onwards (3D S^2 net, Whittaker 1903 wave-kernel bridge, Whittaker 1904 two-scalar-potential bridge, Maxwell scalar-pair reconstruction, full scalar-potential physics) are explicitly staged as separate future cert families [267]-[270]+. Sources: (Whittaker, 1903) On the partial differential equations of mathematical physics, Math. Annalen 57:333-355, DOI 10.1007/BF01444290; primary-source PDF at Documents/whittaker_corpus/whittaker_1903_partial_differential_equations_mathematical_physics.pdf. (Whittaker, 1904) referenced as bridge background only at Documents/whittaker_corpus/whittaker_1904_two_scalar_potential_functions_electromagnetic_field.pdf. Bridge spec docs/specs/QA_WHITTAKER_RATIONAL_DIRECTION_CERT_DRAFT.md. Authored Will Dale + Claude 2026-04-30. Theorem NT compliance: integer-only construction; no x**2 (uses x*x); raw d=b+e, a=b+2e (no mod reduction in element computation); coprime seeds gcd(b,e)=1 enforced; angular ordering via fractions.Fraction(F,C) comparison; floats appear only in observer-side reporting (Lipschitz test grid, sup-error display). Checks WRD_1+DECL/W1/W2/W3/HARD/SRC/WIT/F; 4 PASS + 4 FAIL fixtures; self-test ok
